rule: Any KILLED verdict by a current adversarial judge holds the edge. SURVIVES means the judge could not construct a stronger competitor stack or reduce the value to DX, native packaging, or a mechanism with no measured founder outcome.

verdicts.why: OpenAI or Gemini can reproduce the same fan-out/token-shielding pattern with a single custom execute-script or batch tool that runs the loop in the developer sandbox and returns only a compact result, so Claude's native packaging is DX rather than a durable gap.

A competitor agent runner can combine usage accounting, max output/reasoning/thinking caps, and tool-approval or preflight budget gates to prevent low-budget tool execution on the same workload, making Claude's provider-side countdown nicer but not unmatched.

Prompt-cache counters plus client-side canonical request hashing and section diffs give OpenAI or Gemini users the same practical root-cause diagnosis for model/system/tools/messages changes, so the typed Claude field is convenience observability.

Inline files or extracted page chunks plus structured outputs and a deterministic quote/source resolver give OpenAI or Gemini the no-store mixed-source citation path in practice, leaving Claude with native DX and token-accounting niceties rather than a hard capability gap.

Passing RAG chunks inline with stable IDs and requiring structured chunk/span/quote output lets OpenAI or Gemini return the same zero-hosted-object pointers through a small resolver, so comparing only against hosted File Search overstates the gap.
